reasoning: Iteration 1 regressed significantly with new semantic failures (dictionary outputs '?', aggregates dropped to 50%, selectivity collapsed from 98% to 45%). The attempted fixes introduced additional bugs in delta decoding boundaries and dictionary indexing. With only 1 iteration remaining and multiple independent critical semantic issues requiring careful investigation of binary format/decoding logic, continuing would have very high failure risk with diminishing returns. Recommend manual code review.
strategy_notes: The query is failing correctness validation with three independent semantic defects: (1) dictionary code-to-value mapping producing '?' outputs, (2) date threshold/delta decoding selectivity dropped to 45% (vs expected 98%), (3) aggregate values halved. Iter_1's fixes were incomplete or introduced new bugs. Recommend: (A) manual inspection of lineitem_dicts.txt format and parsing logic, (B) unit test on first 1000 rows to verify date decoding produces correct epoch values, (C) check PerfectHashTable indexing with debug output before re-attempting automated optimization.
notes: GenDB's iter_0 baseline (98.98ms) is already competitive vs DuckDB (86-152ms). The bottleneck is correctness, not performance. Iter_1 proved that semantic fixes require careful validation against actual data format.
